Psychosis is loss of reality in general (like hallucinations typically).
It includes a lot of pathologies and even just big tiredness but schizophrenia is different in a lot of ways than regular psychotic episode:
-You can't cure schizophrenia.
-Cause afflicted people to mostly be violent towards themselves and almost never others (except in breakdown maybe and even then.)
-Schizophrenic peoples are aware that they are psychotic and usually do not show it.
-Lack of emotions, expressing emotions, feeling emotions etc. A lot of people from my experience do theatre to compensate that (me included).
-Pretty similar to autism (you can have both, but it's pretty hard to diagnose since there's a lot of overlap).
-Usually (but not always) accompanied by paranoia or PTSD because it messes with your memory.
-Contrary to popular belief, not caused by drugs. Drugs can amplify it or trigger it though. (Think of having pneumonia with dormant asthma. Doesn't help, but didn't create it.)
-Genetic plays a huge part in having it or not.
-Incoherent speech that is still believable if you don't know the person (sometimes) or just completely incoherent. Not that obvious though.
-Hallucinations can be pretty realistic (or "mild" if you want. Like someone calling you (family), doorbell ringing at home etc). It also is more likely to touch other senses than regular psychosis, it can even be with only "rarer" senses. (like smell or taste).
